items.title: New player experience + Restore Defaults
items.body: Fresh installs now start with a curated setup: Scene Tracker, Present Characters, dialogue coloring, and Discord-style chat bubbles on — everything else off, with the D button centered. Existing setups are untouched. Want the clean slate? Advanced → Restore Default Settings (keeps all your characters, colors, avatars, and presets).

items.title: This screen!
items.body: DES now shows release notes once after every update (desktop and desktop-site view). Dismissing lasts until the next release; turn it off permanently in Display settings. You can reopen it any time with the What's New button in the extensions dropdown.

items.title: Dialogue colors finally stick to the right character
items.body: New characters no longer show up as someone else (or 'Unknown'). DES now learns each speaker's color the moment they debut — validated against the actual message, never guessed — and a manual recolor in the Workshop always wins, with old messages keeping their attribution.

items.title: Context Inspector
items.body: Settings → Context Inspector shows verbatim everything DES injects into the model's context: live slot snapshot plus a per-generation history of every injection, edit, and clear.

items.title: Quick-Jump button (mobile)
items.body: Scroll up in chat to reveal a small button that jumps back to your last message — tap again to walk up through earlier ones. Toggle in Display settings.

items.title: Massive performance rebuild
items.body: Startup payload cut roughly in half; settings UI, modals, and their code load only when first opened; weather particles moved to a single canvas that fully stops when hidden; rendering is incremental instead of rebuilt per message.

items.title: Performance Mode
items.body: New Display toggle that strips all DES animations, blur, and transitions and pauses particles — for minimum GPU/CPU cost on weaker machines.

items.title: Compact Tracker Prompt
items.body: Optional terser tracker instructions (~40% fewer prompt tokens) in Advanced. Existing installs keep the verbose phrasing they were tuned on; fresh installs default to compact.

items.title: Loading intro removed
items.body: The startup cinematic is gone entirely — DES now gets out of the way and loads instantly.
